fundamentals.name: Tower Semiconductor Ltd.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Tower Semiconductor Ltd., an independent semiconductor foundry, provides technology, development, and process platforms for integrated circuits in the United States, Japan, rest of Asia, and Europe. The company provides customizable process technologies, including SiGe, SiPho, mixed signal CMOS, RF CMOS, CMOS image sensor, non-imaging sensors, wafers, and integrated power management. It also offers a design enablement platform for a design cycle, as well as transfer optimization and development process services to integrated device manufacturers and fabless companies. The company serves consumer applications, personal computers, communications, handsets and smartphones, automotive, industrial, aerospace, and medical devices. Tower Semiconductor Ltd. was incorporated in 1993 and is headquartered in Migdal Haemek, Israel

judgment.summary: Tower Semiconductor shows strong growth and profitability but carries high valuation multiples and significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 15.7%; next earnings date is 2026-08-03.
positives: Revenue growth 15.5% YoY and earnings growth 62.9% YoY indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Profit margin at 15.1% and operating margin at 15.6% demonstrate healthy profitability.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (51.6%) and fiscal year (45.9%) are robust.
Strong buy consensus from 6 analysts with a mean target price above the current spot price.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 131.83 and forward P/E of 49.40 are very high, suggesting aggressive growth expectations are priced in.
Debt/Equity ratio of 5.24 indicates significant financial leverage.
Return on equity at 8.7% is modest given the high valuation.
Free cash flow of $128.95M is relatively small compared to the market cap.

news.paragraph: Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) has secured significant growth prospects with recent $1.3 billion in silicon photonics customer contracts, targeting 2027 revenue and anchoring data center and AI expansion. This news, coupled with Wedbush lifting its price target and calling Q1 commentary positive, has driven the stock up nearly 30%. Analysts are bullish, with a consensus 'strong buy' rating and a mean target of $313.83, anticipating continued momentum into the August earnings report.
